title: Abolition of Mandatory Minimum Sentences Act
text:
ABOLITION OF MANDATORY MINIMUM SENTENCES [CH.94A– 1
DERELICT MOTOR VEHICLES (DISPOSAL)
CHAPTER 94A
ABOLITION OF MANDATORY MINIMUM SENTENCES
LIST OF AUTHORISED PAGES
1 – 3 LRO 1/2017
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title.
2. Abolition of mandatory minimum sentences.
–––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
LRO 1/2017 STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS

ABOLITION OF MANDATORY MINIMUM SENTENCES [CH.94A– 3
CHAPTER 94A
ABOLITION OF MANDATORY MINIMUM SENTENCES
An Act to abolish Mandatory Minimum
47 of 2014
Sentences.
[Assent 5th November, 2014]
[Commencement 5 November, 2014]
1. This Act may be cited as the Abolition of Short title.
Mandatory Minimum Sentences Act.
2. Where a provision in any law has the effect of Abolition of
requiring a court to impose a minimum term of mandatory
minimum
imprisonment that provision, to the extent of that
sentences.
requirement, shall be of no effect.
–––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
LRO 1/2017 STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS
